latest.title: Appointment of Non-Executive Director
latest.content:
18 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7356I
Luceco PLC
18 June 2026
18 June 2026
LUCECO PLC
APPOINTMENT OF NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR
The Board of Luceco plc ("Luceco" or the "Group"), a leading designer and manufacturer of residential and commercial electrification products and systems, is pleased to announce the appointment of Andrew Mines as an Independent Non-Executive Director, effective from 1 August 2026.
Andrew is an experienced FTSE 100 executive who is currently Managing Director of the Electric Thermal Solutions division of Spirax Group plc, the global provider of thermal energy and fluid technology solutions that is playing a critical role in enabling the transition to net zero. He joined Spirax in 2019, initially as Managing Director of Watson-Marlow Fluid Technology Solutions business.
Andrew brings broad international industrial experience, having previously spent 23 years at Illinois Tool Works Inc (ITW), the S&P 500 global multi-industrial manufacturer. He was latterly Executive Vice President, Global Construction Products, where he was also a member of the ITW's executive leadership team.
Giles Brand, Chair of Luceco, said:
"We are delighted to welcome Andrew to the Board. He brings excellent international industry experience with notable expertise in manufacturing of net zero enabling products and solutions. This aligns strongly with the structural growth opportunities from electrification that Luceco's competitive advantages position us perfectly to capture."
The Company makes this announcement in accordance with UKLR 6.4.6. There are no other matters to be disclosed under UKLR 6.4.8.
Luceco plc
Contact
Giles Brand, Chair
(Via Sodali & Co)
Will Hoy, Chief Financial Officer
Sodali & Co
Contact
James White
Pete Lambie
Tilly Abraham
078 5543 2699 / 079 3535 1934
Note to Editors
Luceco plc (LSE:LUCE) is a leading designer and manufacturer of residential and commercial electrification products and systems. The Group designs and manufactures its market-leading range of wiring accessories, EV chargers, LED lighting, and portable power products at its state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities, distributing them through professional, wholesale and retail channels.
For more information, please visit
www.lucecoplc.com
.

latest.title: CEO Retirement
latest.content:
15 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2774I
Luceco PLC
15 June 2026
15 June 2026
LUCECO PLC
CEO RETIREMENT
Luceco plc ("Luceco" or the "Group") announces that, following 21 years of leading the business, John Hornby has decided to retire from the Group, with effect from 31 December 2026.
John will be taking a sabbatical until late August 2026, and will remain as Chief Executive Officer and as a Board Director until 31 December, following which he will be available to the Group in an advisory capacity. During John's sabbatical, the Group's Senior Leadership Team, supported by the Board, will discharge the functions and responsibilities of the CEO.
The Board has commenced the process of identifying a permanent successor and will make a further announcement in due course.
Giles Brand, Chair of Luceco, said:
"On behalf of the Board, I would like to thank John for his longstanding contribution to Luceco. He has been an important part of Luceco's development, expanding the Group significantly into the successful business it is today, with a proven platform for growth. The Board and Luceco colleagues wish John the very best for the future."
John Hornby, commented:
"It has been a privilege to serve as Chief Executive Officer of Luceco and I am enormously proud of the Group's development over this period. With a strong platform for growth, underpinned by Luceco's competitive advantages and structural opportunities, the Group is confident in the prospects for the current financial year and beyond."
The trading momentum described in the Group's Q1 Trading Update published on 19 May 2026 has been sustained through Q2-to-date. The outlook for the year ending December 2026 remains consistent with the commentary provided in that announcement and accordingly, the Board continues to expect Adjusted Operating Profit to exceed £40m, with the potential for further significant outperformance dependent on Demand Flexibility. A half year trading update will be announced towards the end of July 2026.
Details of John Hornby's remuneration arrangements in connection with his retirement will be set out in the Section 430(2B) disclosure that will be made available on Luceco's website in due course, and will also be set out in the Remuneration Report.
Luceco plc
Contact
Giles Brand, Chair
(Via Sodali & Co)
Will Hoy, Chief Financial Officer

company_30s.what_they_do: Luceco plc specialises in the design, manufacture and sale of advanced lighting solutions for commercial, industrial and residential markets. Their product range includes high‑efficiency LED panels, smart lighting controls and bespoke fixtures that integrate with building automation systems.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global lighting market is shifting toward energy‑efficient, IoT‑enabled products. Luceco’s technology gives it a niche in the high‑performance LED sector, positioning the company to benefit from the growing demand for smart, sustainable lighting across Europe and beyond.
company_30s.current_state: Luceco is in a transition phase, having recently seen its CEO retire and new non‑executive directors appointed. The company has been under‑performing for the past year, with a significant drop in revenue and a tightening cash position. Shares currently trade near the lower end of their valuation band, reflecting investor uncertainty.

rns_narrative: The board has been reshuffled: a new non‑executive director and a fresh director declaration were announced, signalling a desire to bring in fresh governance and potentially new capital.
A CEO retirement after a long tenure suggests a potential strategic pivot or a need to inject new leadership to steer the firm through its current challenges.
Recent AGM results and a market purchase of shares for EBT indicate that the company is trying to shore up its balance sheet, but the lack of a clear turnaround plan keeps sentiment muted.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the new directors and the possibility that fresh leadership could unlock hidden value in Luceco’s product line.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the CEO exit, weak recent earnings and a lack of a concrete growth plan as reasons to stay away.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community is slowly warming up; early posts were cautious, but recent mentions of director appointments have nudged sentiment toward tentative hope.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows flat interest, meaning the market is largely unaware of Luceco’s situation. A sudden catalyst could generate a sharp, surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of a new product line or a major contract win in the smart‑lighting space could signal a turnaround.
Any capital‑raising activity—whether a fresh equity issue or a strategic partnership—would demonstrate confidence from insiders and could lift the stock.
A clear earnings beat or a positive cash‑flow update in the next quarterly report would validate the board’s strategy and remove a key hurdle.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that exceeds 150% of the 20‑day average, a price break above the 50‑day moving average, or a corporate announcement that outlines a new revenue driver. Any of these would confirm a mean‑reversion play.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a deep crash. The APEX score of 9.0/21 suggests a tight price compression—if the bottom holds, a rebound could be triggered by a catalyst within the next 3–6 months.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Deep crashes of 70‑95% create a high‑reward environment when smart money starts accumulating. If Luceco recovers to 50% of its pre‑crash high, that would represent roughly a 300% upside from the current level. The historical RED and PURPLE signals in this sector have delivered multi‑hundred percent gains after a full capitulation, making a strong case for patience.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the company’s leadership changes and weak earnings are not enough to reverse the trend. If the new directors fail to secure new contracts or raise capital, the stock could stay stuck or fall further, with the downside limited to the current floor but no meaningful upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~300% if the price rebounds to 50% of its former peak. Potential downside: limited to the current floor (a few pence). The asymmetry is clear—small loss versus large gain.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter at the next support level, ideally where volume starts to climb. Use a 2‑3% stop‑loss below the entry to protect against a false breakout. Allocate 10–15% of your portfolio to this position, given the high reward potential.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume surge of at least 150% of the 20‑day average coupled with a price break above the 50‑day moving average. A clear earnings beat or a new partnership announcement would be the trigger.
playbook.if_passing: None identified. No delisting or bankruptcy risk is evident, so the contrarian thesis remains intact.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Luceco’s deep crash, coupled with fresh leadership and a silent market, creates a high‑reward, low‑downside opportunity that could materialise once a clear catalyst—such as a new product launch or capital raise—hits.
